comments.author: ddl-galias
comments.body: If I am not missing anything, this form instance is not reactive. This can lead to issues with the `prevProvider` logic. Have you checked https://ant.design/components/form#formusewatch for watching form values?

comments.author: DDL-Martin-Gazzara
comments.body: Replaced with useWatch

pr_title: DOM-54752 Dom 54752 UI add new endpoint modal

comments.author: ddl-galias
comments.body:
The prev + useEffect pattern is discouraged by the react team: https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#adjusting-some-state-when-a-prop-changes. I don't like the new pattern too much so the change is up to you.

Regardless of the comment above, prevProvider value should not care about `model` value. We should always keep them in sync.

comments.author: DDL-Martin-Gazzara
comments.body: I'll go with the React's piece of advice

pr_title: [DOM-55073] Flows: executor writes outputs success flag file instead of user code

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: What happens in workflows that have no inputs or outputs? Is there any risk that this would fail if `/workflow/outputs/` didn't already exist?

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: this func is never called if there are no outputs. so there is no risk in that respect

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: if there are outputs, then the `/workflow/outputs/` directory will exist because it is mounted as an empty dir by k8s
